assumptions: Formula: netScore = ((2*strongly + somewhat) - (2*oppStrongly + oppSomewhat)) / totalTrials; range -2..+2
Direction: isOpponent = netScore < 0; netScore=0 renders with no fill (tie)
Label: magnitude only, Math.abs(netScore).toFixed(1); no sign character
Color: blue when selected leads, orange when opponent leads, none at tie; opacity = |netScore|/2
Applies to all three views: ConditionMatrix, ConditionDecisionsTable, PivotAnalysisTable
Shared helper canonicalConditionSummary.ts becomes single source of truth
Rename winnerScore -> netScore in helper return shape; update all call sites
ConditionMatrix inline formula deleted; component consumes shared helper

Test fixtures in canonicalConditionSummary.test.ts, ConditionDecisionsTable.test.tsx, AnalysisPanel.test.tsx, PivotAnalysisTable.test.tsx, DomainAnalysisValueDetail.test.tsx updated to match new formula
No snapshot rebuild required: snapshots store raw counts, not computed score
No GraphQL schema change: API exposes raw counts; fix is purely client-side
discovery.summary: All discovery questions pre-resolved during user conversation. See assumptions in state.
non_goals: Changing GraphQL schema or API raw count exposure
Changing per-transcript preferenceScore in CSV/XLSX exports
Changing meanPreferenceScore aggregation in XLSX model summary
acceptance_criteria: Preflight passes: lint + build + tests for shared, db, api, web workspaces
All three views (ConditionMatrix, ConditionDecisionsTable, PivotAnalysisTable) show identical score for identical raw count inputs
3-somewhat-opponent vs 2-somewhat-selected scenario displays 0.2 in faint opponent color (not 0.6 or 1)
2-strongly-each + 1-neutral tie scenario displays 0.0 with no fill
5-strongly-opponent blowout displays 2.0 in full-opacity opponent color
No labels show a sign character (all magnitudes)

parallel_analysis.note: Slices 2 and 3 are independent once Slice 1 lands — both consume the new shared module surface without touching each other's files. They could run in parallel. Leaving sequential for review simplicity (each slice gets its own diff checkpoint). Within slices, no parallelization opportunities.
